data.title: How Much Bitcoin SpaceX Holds After Its Latest $105M Transfer
data.description:
SpaceX has made another notable move on the blockchain, shifting a total of 1,163 BTC, worth roughly $105 million, from one of its internal wallets to two unmarked destinations. The transfers, detected in the early hours of the day through Coinbase Prime flow data, brought fresh attention to the company’s overall digital asset positioning.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/how-much-bitcoin-spacex-holds-after-its-latest-105m-transfer/

data.title: UK Proposes New “No Gain, No Loss” Tax Rules to Simplify DeFi Activity
data.description:
The UK is moving toward one of its most meaningful crypto tax reforms to date, as HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC) proposes a new “no gain, no loss” (NGNL) framework designed to ease the tax burden for decentralized finance users.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/uk-proposes-new-no-gain-no-loss-tax-rules-to-simplify-defi-activity/

data.title: CME Data Indicates High Probability of Fed Rate Cut in December
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, CME's FedWatch tool reveals an 86.9% probability of a 25 basis point rate cut by the Federal Reserve in December, with a 13.1% chance of maintaining the current rate. By January next year, the likelihood of a cumulative 25 basis point cut stands at 67.3%, while the probability of keeping rates unchanged is 9.6%. Additionally, there is a 23.1% chance of a cumulative 50 basis point reduction.

data.title: Bitcoiners accuse JPMorgan of rigging the game against Strategy, DATs
data.description:
The financial services giant has filed with the US Securities and Exchange Commission to launch a leveraged BTC financial product.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoiners-accuse-jpmorgan-rigging-strategy-dats?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss%3Fnc%3D1764280472662%26_rnd%3D879as1&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Bitcoin Community Criticizes JPMorgan's Bitcoin-Backed Notes
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, members of the Bitcoin community and supporters of Strategy, the largest corporate holder of Bitcoin (BTC), are voicing concerns over JPMorgan's proposed Bitcoin-backed notes. The bank is accused of spreading fear, uncertainty, and doubt about Strategy and other crypto treasury firms. JPMorgan's notes are a leveraged investment product linked to the price of Bitcoin, offering holders 1.5 times the gains or losses through December 2028. Scheduled for a December 2025 launch, the product has sparked criticism from the Bitcoin community, which views JPMorgan as a direct competitor to BTC treasury companies. Critics argue that the bank has an incentive to marginalize companies like Strategy to promote its own financial product.

Bitcoin advocate Simon Dixon highlighted that JPMorgan's product could trigger margin calls on Bitcoin-backed loans, potentially forcing sell pressure from Bitcoin treasury companies during market downturns. On social media platform X, crypto enthusiasts and Strategy supporters are calling for a boycott of JPMorgan, urging fellow Bitcoiners to close accounts and sell any shares they might own in the financial services giant.

The backlash against JPMorgan intensified following a proposed policy shift by MSCI, formerly Morgan Stanley Capital International. The company, which manages stock indexes, suggested excluding treasury companies with 50% or more of their assets in cryptocurrencies from its products. Set to take effect in January, this proposal has drawn sharp criticism from the BTC community and Strategy investors. Excluding crypto treasury companies from stock indexes could deprive them of passive capital flows, potentially forcing these companies to sell off their crypto holdings to qualify for index inclusion, thereby driving asset prices down further.

data.title: Bitcoin Approaches a Critical Reversal Zone as Analysts Track $94K-$95K Signal
data.description:
Bitcoin is navigating one of its most delicate phases of the year, with market structure, risk signals, and seasonal volatility colliding across multiple data points. Analysts from Bitcoin Vector, Swissblock, and Bitcoinsensus are highlighting that the market remains in a high-risk regime, but the core trend may finally be stabilizing.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bitcoin-approaches-a-critical-reversal-zone-as-analysts-track-94k-95k-signal/

data.title: U.S. Bank Faces Increased Capital Requirements After FSB Reassessment
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, the Financial Stability Board (FSB) has elevated the systemic importance of a major U.S. bank, resulting in higher capital requirements. The bank has been moved to the third-highest tier on the FSB's global systemically important banks (GSIB) list, increasing its additional capital buffer requirement from 1.5% to 2%. Meanwhile, Deutsche Bank has been downgraded by one tier, reducing its additional capital requirement to 1%. No other changes were made compared to the previous year.

data.title: Wall Street Banks Anticipate Growth in Emerging Markets Amid Dollar Weakness
data.description: According to Odaily, major Wall Street banks are preparing for renewed success in emerging markets, driven by expectations of a weakening dollar and a surge in investments in artificial intelligence. These favorable conditions are anticipated to further boost emerging markets, with local currency bonds offering investors a 7% return, marking the best performance since 2020, and currency indices rising over 6%. Morgan Stanley strategists highlight that as the U.S. economy slows, the Federal Reserve may further cut interest rates, supporting continued market growth. The bank advises clients to maintain long positions in emerging market local currency bonds, projecting an 8% return by mid-2026. For emerging market dollar bonds, they forecast a "high single-digit" increase over the next 12 months.

data.title: Bank of America faces higher additional capital requirements and its systemic importance has been upgraded.
data.description: Bank of America's systemic importance has been upgraded by the Financial Stability Board (FSB), meaning it will face higher capital requirements. In the FSB's list of Global Systemically Important Banks (GSIBs), the bank has been moved to the third highest tier. Its additional capital buffer requirement has increased from 1.5% to 2%. Deutsche Bank was downgraded one tier, with its additional capital requirement decreasing to 1%. Aside from these changes compared to the previous year, there were no other adjustments.

data.title: Wall Street maintains its optimistic outlook for emerging markets in 2026.
data.description: Major Wall Street banks are poised for another strong showing in emerging markets, anticipating further momentum from a weaker dollar and a surge in investment in artificial intelligence. These positive factors are expected to drive further gains in emerging markets, with local currency bonds delivering a 7% return – the best performance since 2020 – and currency indices rising by over 6%. Morgan Stanley strategists point out that the Federal Reserve may further cut interest rates as the US economy slows, which will support the market rally. The bank recommends clients maintain long positions in emerging market local currency bonds, projecting returns of around 8% by mid-2026. For emerging market dollar bonds, they forecast "high single-digit" gains over the next 12 months.

data.title: Blockrise obtains MiCAR license to launch BTC-collateralized lending, plans to raise €15 million.
data.description: Dutch Bitcoin platform Blockrise announced that it has received authorization from the Dutch Financial Markets Authority (AFM) under the EU MiCAR framework to provide Bitcoin-related services in Europe. Blockrise is a "Bitcoin-only" platform that will offer loans to corporate clients secured by Bitcoin, with a minimum loan amount of €20,000. Clients retain ownership of the collateralized BTC, which cannot be re-pledged. The company previously raised €2 million in seed funding and now plans to launch a €15 million Series A round to expand its EU market and lending business.

data.title: Prediction Markets Surge with Kalshi and Polymarket Leading the Way
data.description:
According to Odaily, prediction markets are rapidly emerging as a new trend in the crypto and fintech sectors. Kalshi has secured over $1.3 billion in new capital this quarter, with its valuation soaring from $5 billion to $11 billion in a matter of weeks. Meanwhile, its competitor Polymarket is in discussions for new funding, aiming for a valuation between $12 billion and $15 billion.

The market capital is heavily concentrated on Kalshi and Polymarket, with investors betting on the two platforms forming a duopoly in the prediction market space. They are expected to become central venues for trading event risk and sentiment data. Both platforms are benefiting from a clearer regulatory path in the United States: Kalshi operates as a CFTC-licensed exchange, while Polymarket has recently received CFTC approval to re-enter the U.S. market.

data.title: Kalshi's valuation has doubled in weeks, predicting a duopoly in the market.
data.description: Prediction markets have rapidly become a new hotspot in the crypto and fintech sector, with Kalshi receiving over $1.3 billion in new capital this quarter, its valuation soaring from $5 billion to $11 billion within weeks. Its competitor, Polymarket, is also in talks for new funding, targeting a valuation of $12 billion to $15 billion. Market capital is highly concentrated on the Kalshi and Polymarket platforms, with investors betting that they will form a duopoly in the prediction market, becoming the core venue for trading event risk and sentiment data. Both platforms' momentum benefits from a clearer US regulatory path: Kalshi is a CFTC-licensed exchange, and Polymarket has just received CFTC approval to re-enter the US market.

data.title: Chainlink Reserve Approaches 1 Million LINK as Enterprise Demand Accelerates — Here Is What’s Powering the Surge
data.description:
The Chainlink Reserve is nearing the significant milestone of 1 million LINK tokens, a development that underscores a rapid increase in demand from enterprises looking to leverage decentralized oracle networks. This surge in interest can be attributed to several key factors, including the growing realization among businesses of the value that Chainlink's technology offers in improving data reliability and security for smart contracts, as well as an expanding range of use cases in various industries that highlight the advantages of integrating blockchain solutions. In this context, we will explore the underlying trends and innovations driving this impressive growth in enterprise adoption of Chainlink's services.
source: https://blocknews.com/chainlink-reserve-approaches-1-million-link-as-enterprise-demand-accelerates-here-is-whats-powering-the-surge/
